standard_description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
subject: Language

khan_exercises.title: Identifying nouns
khan_exercises.description: Learn to identify nouns in context.

items.question_content:
**Which words in the sentence are nouns?**

>Howard eats a sandwich.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A noun is a person, place, thing, or idea! 

**Remember:** nouns include proper names.

**"Howard"** is the name of a person, so it's a noun.

A **"sandwich"** is a thing, so it's a noun too.

khan_exercises.title: Singular and plural nouns
khan_exercises.description: Distinguish singular and plural nouns from one another!

items.question_content:
**Which of the nouns in this sentence are plural?**

>Arif enchanted the Queen’s blade with many powerful spells.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A noun is **singular** when it refers to one thing. 

A noun is **plural** when it refers to *more* than one thing. Plural nouns *usually* end in 's'.
**"Spells"** is a plural noun. Notice how the sentence says there are "many" spells.

items.question_content:
**Which of the nouns in this sentence are plural?**

>Reuben put on goggles to protect his eyes.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A noun is **singular** when it refers to one thing. 

A noun is **plural** when it refers to *more* than one thing. Plural nouns *usually* end in 's'.
**"Goggles"** and **"eyes"** are both plural nouns.

items.question_content:
**Which word in this sentence is a verb?**

>Jake lifted the chrome panel.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Verbs are **actions**.  Which word in this sentence tells you **what action is being done**?
The action being done in this sentence is **lifting**. The verb is **"lifted"**.

khan_exercises.title: Introduction to verb agreement
khan_exercises.description: Choose the appropriate form of the verb by asking yourself: "How many things is the sentence about?"

items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank with the verb that fits best.**

>We  \_\_\_\_\_\_ at dawn.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

For example:

* The elephant **eats**.
* The elephants **eat**.
In this sentence, **"we"** is a plural subject. It needs a plural verb, so the correct form is **"leave"**.

items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank with the verb that fits best.**

>Muneera  \_\_\_\_\_\_ a painting on the wall.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

For example:

* The elephant **eats**.
* The elephants **eat**.
In this sentence, **"Muneera"** is a singular subject. A singular subject needs a singular verb, so the correct form is **"adjusts"**.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

For example:

* The elephant **eats**.
* The elephants **eat**.
In this sentence, **"violinist"** is a singular subject. A singular subject needs a singular verb, so the correct form is **"lifts"**.

items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank with the verb that fits best.**

>In the stream, the school of minnows  \_\_\_\_\_\_ away from Adrian's feet.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Verbs** must *agree* with their **subjects**. So plural subjects (like *we*, *they*, or *elephants*) need plural verbs, while singular subjects (like *I*, *you*, or *elephant*) need singular verbs.

For example:

* The elephant **eats**.
* The elephants **eat**.
In this sentence, **"school"** is a singular subject. A singular subject needs a singular verb, so the correct form is **"darts"**. Don't be thrown off by the "of minnows" part!
